assertions.text: paid-status-on-entity: 'paid' added to OrderStatus and markAsPaid() method added
assertions.evidence: 'paid' key in OrderStatus const; markAsPaid() method mirrors cancel() pattern
use-cases/mark-order-as-paid.ts exists, wraps execute() in telemetry.span(...)

assertions.text: consumer-delegates-not-implements-logic: only parses and calls execute()
assertions.evidence: No direct DB access; 2 execute() calls; dispatches via switch on event type

assertions.text: consumer-skip-on-error: try/catch per-message, logs not throws
assertions.evidence: 2 try/catch blocks in eachMessage handler (one for parse, one for dispatch)

assertions.text: main-ts-runs-both: main.ts calls both HTTP and consumer bootstraps
assertions.evidence: bootstrapExpress() and bootstrapKafkaPaymentConsumer() both called in main.ts

assertions.text: http-adapter-untouched: Express controller and bootstrap not modified

qualitative_notes: Without-skill created an unnecessary IMessagingConsumerPort abstract class in application/ports/inbound/messaging.ts (a single-method 'start()' interface). The skill explicitly documents this as over-abstraction — no port is needed between the consumer and the use case. This is a design smell that adds complexity without benefit, but does not fail any of the quantitative assertions.
